summary: Verified NEW backend + frontend features: legacy OTP endpoints removed (404), Firebase verify still 401 on bogus, Media library upload/list/serve, Site-assets set/clear + public map + slot validation, Events CRUD + past-ended filtering, admin auth gate on all new endpoints. Frontend: admin nav shows Media/Site Images/Events, event create → strip + card render on homepage, media upload via hidden input works and thumbnails load (200 image), site-assets MediaPicker assigns image to slot and thumbnail updates.

ui_bugs.issue: data-testid="admin-media-upload" is on the visible <button>, not on the hidden <input type="file">. Playwright page.set_input_files on the button fails with 'Node is not an HTMLInputElement'. Review request explicitly asked for the testid to be on the hidden file input for automation. Workaround used: page.locator('input[type="file"]').first — worked. Suggest either moving the testid to the input, or adding a second testid like admin-media-upload-input on the <input>.

verifications.site_assets_public_after_set: home_hero_1 present in GET /api/site-assets, disappears after PUT {media_id:null}

action_items: (LOW) Move data-testid='admin-media-upload' to the hidden <input type='file'> in /app/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminMedia.jsx (or add a companion testid). Current placement on the button prevents Playwright set_input_files without a fallback selector.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminMedia.jsx: the file input is the actual upload target — put data-testid there. Buttons that only trigger .click() can have a different testid (e.g. admin-media-upload-btn).
Slot regex '^[a-z0-9_\-]{2,64}$' is good, but consider also rejecting leading/trailing underscores or hyphens to keep slot keys tidy.

site_assets_public strips slots with null url — consider returning them anyway as null so the frontend can distinguish 'known slot cleared' vs 'unknown slot' (currently the client must know all slot keys itself).
Emergent storage init log 'Object storage initialised' should be verified on startup; storage endpoints returned image bytes correctly during tests, so init worked.
test_credentials: admin@gemora.in / admin@1234 (owner role, password login used)
seed_data_creation: Uploaded a handful of 1x1 PNGs to media library during tests; cleaned up the Diwali/Playwright/Expired events and cleared home_hero_1 slot post-run. Some test PNGs remain in the media library (visible in AdminMedia screenshot) — safe to leave or delete.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new endpoints under /api/admin/{media,site-assets,events} plus public /api/site-assets and /api/events/active are wired correctly and gated by require_admin. Frontend admin nav & pages render for owner (admin@gemora.in). Storage backend is real (Emergent object storage). The only nit is that AdminMedia.jsx puts the data-testid on the upload button instead of the hidden file input — the review request explicitly asked the reverse. Fix is 1 line.
